authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Fund Transfers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary CFPB compliance resource for Regulation E deposit-account servicing topics including disclosures, consumer liability, error resolution, and examination materials relevant to retail banking support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retail-banking request intake, dispute classification, and consumer-protection grounding

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1005.11 Procedures for Resolving Errors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative Regulation E rule text for notice, investigation, provisional-credit, correction, and customer-notification timing in electronic fund transfer error handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Debit-card or ACH dispute support, provisional-credit status handling, and error-resolution communication

authoritative_sources.title: Regulation CC, Availability of Funds and Collection of Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Federal Reserve consumer-compliance handbook chapter defining funds-availability schedules, hold notices, exception holds, and collection-of-checks handling used in deposit support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Funds-availability, hold-notice, and deposit exception support handling

authoritative_sources.title: HelpWithMyBank.gov
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCC customer-assistance site describing complaint and inquiry support for national banks and federal savings associations, which anchors complaint intake and escalation expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Complaint intake, customer communication, and regulated-bank support routing

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Reporting Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency BSA/AML manual content used by banking regulators to evaluate suspicious-activity reporting controls and confidentiality boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fraud, AML, suspicious-activity triage, and disclosure-safe support handling

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Report Supporting Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FinCEN guidance clarifying handling of SAR-supporting documentation and confidentiality expectations for financial institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sensitive evidence handling and refusal to disclose investigative posture

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Management Topics: Fraud Monitoring Phase 2
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized ACH operating-rules source that defines risk-based fraud-monitoring and unauthorized or false-pretense entry handling expectations for ACH participants including RDFIs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ACH fraud, return, and suspicious-credit support handling

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Services Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized financial-message vocabulary standard used to normalize account, transaction, and payment artifacts across core-banking, card, payment, and GL systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system transaction normalization and reconciliation review

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and account or transaction grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm customer, account, card, transaction, product, jurisdiction, and service-channel identifiers before acting and determine whether the request is informational, complaint-driven, exception-driven, or owner-transfer sensitive.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Record-of-truth and evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Verify core-banking records, card or ACH events, servicing notes, complaint history, hold notices, and communication logs before stating status or next steps.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution and safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Respond using only documented workflow state, dispute timing, hold posture, and next-owner boundaries without promising refunds, reversals, suspicious-activity clearance, or approvals.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, and repeat-failure escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package complaints, unsupported asks, unresolved prerequisites, and repeat servicing failures for the correct owner with an auditable communication trail.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and suspicious-activity boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when fraud indicators, mule-account patterns, suspicious credits, KYC issues, or AML-sensitive facts materially change the answer and do not disclose SAR posture or investigative status.

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, reconciliation, and tenant-adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Stop short of finality when disclosure dates, hold notices, dispute notifications, or transaction records conflict across systems or when tenant policy is stricter than the public baseline.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

artifacts: account profile, CIF, customer communication logs, and servicing notes
ledger and memo-posted transactions, available-balance records, hold notices, and statement history
debit-card, ACH, check, dispute, stop-payment, and provisional-credit artifacts
complaint records, case-management notes, escalation packets, and approval-history artifacts
KYC, CIP, fraud-monitoring, AML, suspicious-activity, and privacy-sensitive evidence markers
general-ledger, reconciliation, and payment-network records used to establish transaction truth
systems_of_record: card processor or debit network case system
fraud monitoring and AML review systems

allowed: Resolve informational retail-banking support requests when the request can be answered from authoritative records and documented workflow rules.
Request the minimum missing identifiers, records, and tenant facts needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed communication summaries, resolution notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route the case to the correct adjacent owner when the workflow becomes fraud, AML, legal, underwriting, card operations, or discretionary approval work.
escalate: Required tenant facts, identity context, or source records are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks a policy exception, fee reversal, hold release, account approval, or dispute outcome outside documented service authority.
Fraud, AML, suspicious-activity, or consumer-impact complaint signals appear.
Public guidance and tenant policy materially disagree on what may be communicated, credited, reversed, or disclosed.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, regulated advice, underwriting, or approval authority.
Requests to fabricate approvals, assert unsupported transaction truth, or disclose SAR or investigative posture.
Requests to bypass documented complaint, dispute, fraud, KYC, AML, or privacy controls.

domain_failure_modes: telling the customer a transaction error is resolved when core, card, or ACH records still conflict
promising fee reversal, hold release, or account reopening without the documented approval owner
disclosing or implying suspicious-activity or SAR status during ordinary support communication
treating a fraud or mule-account signal as ordinary service friction and continuing with customer accommodation
closing repeat complaints without an owner-routed escalation package and communication log
using generic customer-service wording that omits dispute timing, hold notice, or account-record dependencies
failing to reconcile transaction, disclosure, and complaint records before asserting final customer impact

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific thresholds for fee-reversal authority, hold exceptions, and complaint-routing SLAs
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Treat missing thresholds as escalation-only and do not imply approval authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product and jurisdiction differences across account agreements, state laws, and network or processor procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require tenant product, jurisdiction, and processor context at runtime before applying a final servicing posture.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, complaint handling, suspicious-activity boundaries, and reconciliation controls instead of only consumer-protection law.

The lane stops at retail-banking support and resolution. Final dispute decisions, discretionary fee credits, fraud findings, SAR decisions, underwriting outcomes, and legal interpretation remain outside the lane.
